name: Cabaña Verónica from Fuente Dé through Horcados Rojos – Picos de Europa National Park (PR-PNPE 23)
name_original: Cabaña Verónica desde Fuente Dé por Horcados Rojos – Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa (PR-PNPE 23)
region_intro: Hiking around Camaleño offers access to the Cantabrian sector of the Picos de Europa National Park, characterized by dramatic limestone walls, deep gorges, and high mountain pastures. The region features picturesque valleys, including the Valle de Camaleño, and is home to Fuente Dé, a glacial cirque where the Deva River originates. The Fuente Dé Cable Car provides quick ascent to high-altitude areas, serving as a starting point for numerous trails. Best hiking trails around Camaleño The most popular hiking route is Puertos de Áliva from Fuente Dé — circular in the Parque Nacional Picos de Europa, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route offers mountain views and varied scenery, including grasslands and forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabaña Verónica from Fuente Dé through Horcados Rojos – Picos de Europa National Park (PR-PNPE 23), a difficult 9.4 miles (15.1 km) path. It provides challenging terrain and vistas within the Picos de Europa National Park. Local hikers also love the Bajo los Picos Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through beautiful pastures and villages, often completed in about 2 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Camaleño is defined by dramatic mountain scenery of the Picos de Europa, picturesque valleys, and access to high-altitude trails via the Fuente Dé Cable Car. The network offers options for easy family-friendly walks to challenging high-mountain treks. The routes in Camaleño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 3,000 reviews. More than 18,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Camaleño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Camaleño?
A: Camaleño offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with nearly 150 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls through valleys to challenging high-mountain treks within the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Camaleño has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1195755525/bajo-los-picos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bajo los Picos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.1 km route that leads through beautiful pastures and villages, offering a pleasant experience. The region also features routes like the Brez – Canal de las Arredondas Circuit Trail, an easy 2-hour circuit with fantastic views.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Camaleño?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Camaleño are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1181788544/puertos-de-aliva-from-fuente-de-circular-in-the-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Áliva from Fuente Dé — circular in the Parque Nacional Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning mountain views and varied scenery.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Camaleño is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the dramatic limestone walls and deep gorges of the Picos de Europa National Park. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Dé Cable Car&lt;/a&gt;, which provides access to high-altitude areas and breathtaking panoramic views. Other highlights include the picturesque Valle de Camaleño and charming villages like Mogrovejo.

Q: Is the Fuente Dé Cable Car a good starting point for hikes?
A: Yes, the Fuente Dé Cable Car is an excellent starting point, especially for accessing high-altitude trails quickly. It ascends visitors 753 meters to an altitude of 1,823 meters in just four minutes, opening up numerous routes that would otherwise require significant uphill trekking. Many popular trails, such as those leading to Puertos de Áliva, begin from the top station.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking options in Camaleño?
A: While the Picos de Europa National Park has specific regulations regarding dogs, many trails in the broader Camaleño region and its valleys are suitable for hiking with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash. Always check local signage and park rules for specific restrictions, especially in protected areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Camaleño?
A: The best time for hiking in Camaleño is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is milder, and the high-mountain trails are more accessible. Summer offers warm, sunny days, while spring and autumn provide lush landscapes and vibrant colors, though some higher passes might still have snow early in the season.

Q: Are there options for challenging, high-mountain treks?
A: Yes, Camaleño is a gateway to the Picos de Europa National Park, offering numerous challenging high-mountain treks. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996631656/cabana-veronica-from-fuente-de-through-horcados-rojos-picos-de-europa-national-park-pr-pnpe-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabaña Verónica from Fuente Dé through Horcados Rojos&lt;/a&gt; provide demanding terrain and stunning vistas for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996658326/vega-de-liordes-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Liordes Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is another longer, hard hike with significant elevation gain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Camaleño?
A: The hiking trails in Camaleño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 3,000 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic mountain scenery of the Picos de Europa, the accessibility provided by the Fuente Dé Cable Car, and the diverse range of trails catering to all abilities.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by hiking?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its breathtaking views. The top of the Fuente Dé Cable Car offers panoramic vistas of the surrounding mountains. Many trails lead to spectacular viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing unparalleled perspectives of peaks like Tesorero and Horcados Rojos.

Q: What kind of wildlife and flora can I expect to see?
A: Camaleño boasts a rich and diverse natural environment. Hikers often encounter various bird species, ibex, and other mountain animals. The trails frequently pass through lush beech and oak groves, especially in the valleys, adding to the scenic beauty.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: Public transport options to reach trailheads in Camaleño, particularly to key locations like Fuente Dé, are available, though they might be limited depending on the season. Buses connect larger towns like Potes with Fuente Dé. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance, especially if you plan to rely on public transport for your hiking adventures.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Picos de Europa National Park?
A: Generally, day hiking in the Picos de Europa National Park does not require specific permits for access to most trails. However, certain activities like overnight stays in specific areas, climbing, or large group events might require prior authorization. Always consult the official Picos de Europa National Park website for the most current regulations before your visit.
tips: El Cable is the top station of the Fuente Dé cable car. The mechanical device is a kilometer and a half long, overcomes a drop of 750 meters in four minutes and leaves passengers at an altitude of 1,820 meters. The views are spectacular. In summer and while the mountain is snow-free, there is the possibility of walking up from the Fuente Dé car park through El Hachero, a dizzying path along an old mining road that ends at the balconies of El Cable. And there is also the option of the Canal de la Jendunda, which is a huge gutter on the left wall (west) of the great wall that forms the natural circus of the source of the Deva river. In winter these routes can be complicated by snow or ice and the best option is to use the cable car service to start the mountain crossing on the edge of the high Cantabrian mountains. In this case, check the schedules for the return.

This viewpoint offers incredible views from where you can see the cable to Fuente De and several other routes. It is worth stopping to see the path from above and take the opportunity to rest a little!

This is a steep area full of chasms with which we will have to take special care.

There is a descent through a crack, which is not complicated if done carefully. Better to do it without a backpack, although it can also be done with it. Once you pass it, you will see the high!

"Welcome to Planet Veronica!" What Jorge, the guard who receives anyone who wants to board this aircraft carrier/cabin in Picos, usually says. Nearby there are also bivouac areas and a unique rocky setting.

The cabin is the highest of all the Picos de Europa so it offers a unique perspective. Getting up early is a must to see a unique sunrise!
